publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 166, folios 54-56. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: Dearest W. I am amazingly amused about Master Locke. How cunning of him so artfully & insidiously to throw the whole onus of a non-reconciliation upon you. And I have no doubt that he does just similarly with the family, who thus make long faces & shake their heads over

unforgiving hardened brothers. Were Locke really sorry & really anxious, he would come forward at once with a handsome apology; or ask you frankly & kindly for an explanation on those points where he thinks you have erred, which he perhaps sincerely does imagine.
Locock has changed his tactics, & my mother has just been bled with 12 leeches & cupping-glasses afterwards. I believe about 6 ounces have been

taken. She is certainly relieved considerably by this & not in the smallest degree lowered. Pulse very full & strong, altho' she has besides been taking very lowering medicines, & eaten barely anything. I hope this bleeding may be effectual; but I doubt whether it ought not to have been double. At any rate things are far more comfortable & hopeful.
I can say nothing as yet about returning to Ockham. I cannot say

that the remaining in this way in Town is by any means a temptation to me. I cannot even attend to my own useful & necessary objects. And I could do more in one day that I came up on purpose, between breakfast & dinner, than I can in a week under present circumstances. Not that this is any annoyance to me. I cam up entirely for the Hen, & that object is answered.
Think of my great disappointment when I went

after the Thrushes on Saturday (after I had finished my letters), to find the cages removed, & that no one knew anything of the people, or if they would come again. And I have in vain looked every day for them. I am dying for a pair of thrushes.
I like your account of everything very much indeed.
